title: Lock down Stripe checkout price and return URL handling
description: Checkout currently accepts any client-supplied price_ ID and uses the Origin header to build success/cancel URLs. Restrict accepted prices to server-side allowlisted env/config values, derive return URLs from a trusted site URL, and add abuse/rate controls.

title: Escape beta signup data before rendering email HTML
description: Beta signup fields are interpolated into Resend HTML templates. Escape or template all user-controlled values before insertion, and add tests for HTML/script payloads in first name, last name, email, and biggest frustration.

title: Phase 2: Use case landing pages — /solutions/[slug] ×10
description:
Build 10 SEO-targeted solution landing pages as the primary organic traffic driver for non-branded searches. This is the highest-ROI content investment for the website.

Work included:
- Created src/content/solutions/types.ts (Solution interface)
- Created src/content/solutions/index.ts with all 10 fully-written solution data files
- Created 7 solution components: SolutionHero, SolutionProblem, SolutionHowItHelps, SolutionBenefits, SolutionExample, SolutionFAQ, SolutionCTA, RelatedSolutions
- Created /solutions/[slug]/page.tsx with generateStaticParams, generateMetadata, FAQ JSON-LD, Breadcrumb JSON-LD
- Created /solutions/page.tsx (solutions index)

Solution pages built:
1. /solutions/warranty-tracker
2. /solutions/open-enrollment
3. /solutions/insurance-savings
4. /solutions/employee-benefits
5. /solutions/subscription-management
6. /solutions/credit-card-benefits
7. /solutions/life-reminders
8. /solutions/hsa-fsa-management
9. /solutions/travel-protection
10. /solutions/family-financial-organization

**Status: IMPLEMENTED, pending review**

title: Phase 1: SEO Foundation — JSON-LD, analytics, sitemap, nav
description:
Implement all foundational SEO infrastructure required before content pages can be effective. This is the highest-priority phase as it affects all existing and new pages.

Work included:
- Created src/lib/seo/structured-data.ts with JSON-LD builders (Organization, FAQ, Article, Breadcrumb, Product)
- Updated layout.tsx with GA4 + Microsoft Clarity scripts (env-gated)
- Added Organization JSON-LD globally in layout head
- Updated sitemap.ts to dynamically include solutions, blog, glossary routes
- Updated robots.ts to explicitly allow AI crawlers (GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot)
- Removed /pricing → / redirect from next.config.ts
- Populated Nav with Solutions, Features, Pricing, Blog links
- Expanded Footer with full site navigation
- Added GA/Clarity env vars to .env.example

**Status: IMPLEMENTED, pending review**

title: Stripe and Resend instantiated at module level — breaks CI build
description: Stripe and Resend clients were initialized at module level rather than inside the POST handler. Next.js throws 'Neither apiKey nor config.authenticator provided' during the build phase when STRIPE_SECRET_KEY / RESEND_API_KEY are absent in CI. Fixed by moving instantiation inside the handler so clients only initialize at request time.
close_reason: Fixed: moved Stripe/Resend instantiation inside POST handlers in checkout/route.ts and beta-signup/route.ts. Committed 1b0d0d1.

title: Add API rate limiting and security event logging
description: Public POST endpoints rely on validation and Turnstile but have no explicit rate limiting, IP/user-agent telemetry, structured security logging, or alerting for abuse. Add rate limits for beta signup and checkout, log validation/CAPTCHA/Stripe failures without sensitive values, and wire alerts for spikes.

title: Add dependency vulnerability scanning gate
description: npm audit reports moderate production findings through Next bundled PostCSS, and CI currently runs lint/typecheck/build/e2e but not a dependency vulnerability gate. Add an allowlisted audit or SCA job and track framework upgrades/remediations.

title: Add production HTTP security headers and CSP
description: next.config.ts does not define global security headers. Add HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy, frame protections, and a report-only then enforced CSP that accounts for Next scripts, analytics, Turnstile, Stripe, Supabase, Google Fonts, and Clarity.

title: Security assessment of production readiness
description: Perform a CISO-style security assessment of the repository, including code, dependencies, environment handling, auth/data flows, CI/CD, and production-readiness recommendations.
close_reason: Security assessment completed; follow-up remediation issues filed.

title: Register Google Search Console and submit sitemap
description:
After deploying Phase 1, complete the Google Search Console setup to start indexing all new pages.

Steps:
1. Go to search.google.com/search-console
2. Add property for lifq.ai
3. Verify ownership via DNS TXT record (add in Cloudflare)
4. Submit sitemap at https://lifq.ai/sitemap.xml
5. Request indexing for key pages: /, /solutions, /features, /faq, /blog
6. Monitor Coverage report for any crawl errors

This is required before any of the new pages will rank.

title: Add GA4 + Microsoft Clarity project IDs to Vercel env vars
description:
After the Phase 1 code ships, wire up the actual analytics IDs in Vercel.

Steps:
1. Create a Google Analytics 4 property at analytics.google.com → copy Measurement ID (format: G-XXXXXXXXXX)
2. Create a Microsoft Clarity project at clarity.microsoft.com → copy Project ID
3. Add both to Vercel project environment variables:
   - NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_MEASUREMENT_ID=G-XXXXXXXXXX
   - NEXT_PUBLIC_CLARITY_PROJECT_ID=your-id
4. Verify in GA4 DebugView and Clarity dashboard after deploying

Note: Both are env-gated — if vars are empty, scripts don't load. Safe to deploy without them.

title: Phase 4: Blog + glossary content platform
description:
Build the content velocity engine for topical authority. Blog and glossary are key for AI crawler indexability and long-tail SEO.

Work included:
- Created src/content/blog/index.ts with 3 initial posts (TypeScript data model):
  1. 'What is open enrollment' (7 min read)
  2. 'Hidden credit card benefits' (8 min read)
  3. 'Household insurance audit' (9 min read)
- Created /blog/page.tsx (index with category tags, reading time)
- Created /blog/[slug]/page.tsx (post template with Article JSON-LD, related solutions, CTA)
- Created src/content/glossary/index.ts with 3 initial terms:
  1. 'Open enrollment'
  2. 'HSA (Health Savings Account)'
  3. 'Coverage gap'
- Created /glossary/page.tsx (index)
- Created /glossary/[slug]/page.tsx (term definition with FAQ JSON-LD, examples, related solutions)
- Created src/content/knowledge-base/facts.ts (extracted from knowledge/LifQ.pdf)

**Next steps:**
- Add more blog posts (target: 3+/week)
- Expand glossary (target: 20+ terms)
- Add /guides/[slug] section

**Status: IMPLEMENTED, pending review**

title: Phase 3: New top-level pages — /features, /about, /faq, /contact
description:
Create the core marketing pages needed to complete site navigation, support SEO for branded queries, and establish trust.

Work included:
- /features: 5-pillar feature breakdown, comparison table vs competitors, Product JSON-LD
- /about: Mission, vision, market stats, values, founder context
- /faq: 16 Q&As across 4 categories, FAQ JSON-LD schema
- /contact: Two contact cards (email + beta apply)

All pages include: proper metadata, OpenGraph, canonical URLs, Breadcrumb JSON-LD

**Status: IMPLEMENTED, pending review**

title: Phase 6: Homepage enhancement — new messaging + sections
description:
Update the homepage to reflect the full product positioning ('Personal Protection Operating System') and add missing sections.

Changes needed:
- Update Hero messaging to new positioning
- Add FAQ section above FinalCTA (10 questions, collapsible)
- Add Testimonials section (placeholder cards × 3)
- Update Vision section messaging
- Update FinalCTA to cross-link to /solutions
- Add internal links to solutions and blog from homepage sections

**Status: NOT STARTED**

**Dependencies:** Phases 1-4 should be reviewed and merged first.

title: Phase 5: Tools architecture — /tools/[slug] with calculators
description:
Build interactive, crawlable tool pages that drive organic traffic and generate leads through practical utility.

Tools to build:
1. /tools/insurance-savings-calculator — Input: policy types + spend → estimated savings
2. /tools/benefits-checklist — Printable employer benefits audit checklist
3. /tools/warranty-tracker — Static guide + LifQ CTA

Requirements:
- All tool pages must have SEO copy and be crawlable (content in HTML, not JS-only)
- Interactive calculator portion uses client components
- Full metadata, canonical URLs, structured data
- Internal links to related solutions and blog posts

**Status: NOT STARTED**

description: src/components/layout/Footer.tsx — remove all links to pages that redirect away.

title: Create coming-soon branch from master
description: git checkout -b coming-soon && git push -u origin coming-soon

title: Add /privacy and /terms redirects to next.config.ts
description: Non-permanent redirects to / so no dead routes exist on the coming-soon branch.

title: Set Vercel production branch to coming-soon
description: Manual: Vercel → Project Settings → Git → Production Branch → coming-soon

title: Simplify Nav to logo only
description: src/components/layout/Nav.tsx — remove all links. CTA is on the page itself.

title: Coming Soon Page
description: Deploy a minimal coming-soon page to lifq.ai while the full marketing site continues development on master. Uses a dedicated coming-soon branch so switching to the full site is a single Vercel production branch change.

title: Add page-level event tracking to all key pages
description: homepage (landing_page_viewed via HomePageTracker), pricing (pricing_viewed in useEffect), beta-signup (signup_started on mount, signup_completed after successful POST, cta_clicked for nav/hero/final_cta buttons).

title: Wire PostHogProvider into root layout
description: Wrap body children in layout.tsx with PostHogProvider so all pages have PostHog context.

title: Set NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY in Vercel environment variables
description: Manual step: copy PostHog project key from PostHog → Project Settings and add to Vercel Dashboard → Project Settings → Environment Variables. Same key as LifQ mobile app.

title: Create TrackedCTA component
description: src/components/ui/tracked-cta.tsx — client wrapper around ButtonLink that fires analytics.ctaClicked(location) on click. Used in Hero, FinalCTA, and pricing page.

title: Create HomePageTracker client component
description: src/components/home/HomePageTracker.tsx — null-rendering 'use client' component that fires analytics.landingPageViewed() in useEffect. Workaround for Server Component homepage.

title: Create analytics.ts event helpers
description: src/lib/analytics.ts — typed helpers: landingPageViewed, ctaClicked(location), pricingViewed, signupStarted, signupCompleted. All guard against typeof window === 'undefined'.

title: Create PostHogProvider component (no replays, no autocapture)
description: src/components/providers/PostHogProvider.tsx — client component that initializes posthog-js with disable_session_recording, autocapture: false, capture_pageview: false. Wraps app in PHProvider.

title: PostHog Analytics Integration
description: Wire up PostHog with 5 custom events (landing_page_viewed, cta_clicked, pricing_viewed, signup_started, signup_completed). No session replays, no autocapture. Shared PostHog project with the LifQ mobile app for a unified funnel view.

title: Reconnect Vercel from GitLab to GitHub mirror repo; remove VERCEL_TOKEN/ORG_ID/PROJECT_ID from GitLab CI/CD variables
description: After mirror is verified, reconnect Vercel to GitHub repo in Project Settings → Git. Set production branch to master. Delete the three Vercel secrets from GitLab CI/CD variables.

title: Configure GitLab → GitHub push mirror (Settings → Repository → Mirroring) and verify all branches appear on GitHub
description: Create empty GitHub repo, generate PAT with repo scope, add push mirror in GitLab. Mirror all branches so Vercel sees feature branches for preview deployments.

title: Verify pre-commit hook: stage file with lint error → commit blocked; --no-verify bypasses
description: Manual smoke test of the hook. Confirms escape hatch works for WIP commits.

title: Add lint-staged config to package.json: ESLint --fix + tsc --noEmit on *.{ts,tsx}
description: Runs only on staged files. tsc must check whole project (TypeScript limitation). ESLint auto-fixes trivial issues.

title: Install husky + lint-staged; run npx husky init
description: husky init creates .husky/ and adds prepare script to package.json.

title: Configure .husky/pre-commit to run npx lint-staged
description: Replace default npm test hook with lint-staged.

title: Add e2e job to .gitlab-ci.yml using mcr.microsoft.com/playwright:v1.60.0-jammy; remove deploy jobs
description: e2e job uses build artifact (.next/), starts prod server, runs Playwright. Vercel deploy jobs removed — deployment now via GitHub mirror.

title: Add test:e2e, test:e2e:ui, test:e2e:report scripts to package.json; fix start script to --port 3001
description: start script must specify port 3001 so CI npm run start listens on the same port Playwright targets.

title: Add Playwright output dirs to .gitignore (/playwright-report/, /test-results/, /e2e/.auth/)
description: Prevent generated test artifacts from being committed.

title: Create e2e/pricing.spec.ts — 3 plan cards, billing toggle, Most popular badge
description: Validates pricing page renders all plans and the Annual/Monthly toggle is interactive.

title: Create e2e/beta-signup.spec.ts — form fields, honeypot hidden, validation, submit button
description: Validates form is accessible, honeypot invisible, client-side validation fires on empty submit.

title: Create e2e/home.spec.ts — homepage renders, hero copy, AppShowcase, FinalCTA, CTA link
description: Validates the homepage does not regress on core sections visible to first-time visitors.

title: Create e2e/nav.spec.ts — Pricing link, Log in link, Join the beta, logo nav
description: Validates all nav links have correct hrefs and the logo returns to homepage.

title: Install @playwright/test and run npx playwright install chromium
description: Add @playwright/test to devDependencies, install Chromium browser binary locally for dev runs.

title: Create playwright.config.ts with baseURL env var, chromium-only, CI retries, webServer for local dev
description: Config reads PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL env var; when unset and not in CI, auto-starts dev server via webServer block.

title: E2E Testing & Dev Quality Gates
description: Install Playwright for automated browser testing of all key pages. Add Husky + lint-staged pre-commit hooks so lint and type errors are caught before they reach CI. Restructure GitLab CI to remove Vercel deploy jobs (deploy moves to GitHub mirror → Vercel auto-deploy).

value: SEO revamp started May 2026. Phase 1 (SEO foundation) + Phase 2 (10 solution pages) + Phase 3 (features/about/faq/contact) + Phase 4 (blog+glossary) all implemented in one session. All content in TypeScript data files under src/content/. No MDX packages needed yet. Knowledge base extracted from knowledge/LifQ.pdf into src/content/knowledge-base/facts.ts. GA4 and Clarity are env-gated (NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_MEASUREMENT_ID, NEXT_PUBLIC_CLARITY_PROJECT_ID) — need real IDs added to Vercel.
